name: live extraction shapes: normalized 'Rat' mention still resolves to Ricky
description: Field-observed shapes from the owner's device (grok-4.3, note-extract-v2), NOT idealized ones: the model normalizes the reference mention 'the rat' to the bare proper name 'Rat' (kind 'animal', surface_text 'The rat'), introduces Bella with only a name attribute (no Me.owns edge), and introduces Ricky with ONE Summer.owns relationship whose statement is the only place his rat-ness appears (his kind comes back 'pet', and he has no attribute facts at all). The resolver must still land note 3's 'ate' event on Ricky: reference shape read from surface_text when the name is normalized, and noun matching that sees relationship-edge statements where the entity is the object.
xfail: model normalizes 'the rat' to mention name 'Rat', so parse_reference (name-only) never sees a reference shape and the definite hop never fires; even if it fired, the only rat-evidence lives in Summer's owns-edge STATEMENT (Ricky's kind is 'pet' and he has no attribute facts), which _NOUN_MATCH ignores — a provisional 'Rat' entity is minted and the ate event forks onto it

facts.statement: Summer owns a rat named Ricky.

steps.body: The rat ate the dogs food

facts.statement: The rat ate the dog's food.
Ricky did not eat Bella's food.
